cumulative_with_rounds_1_5: 23 bugs fixed total (1 critical + 9 high + 10 med + 3 low)

rounds.angle: WeakMap / WeakRef / Iterator opaque type collapse
rounds.severity: LOW (forensic — uncommon in game state but defensive)
rounds.evidence: canonicalize({x: new WeakMap()}) === canonicalize({x: {}}) === canonicalize({x: new WeakRef({})}) — all three opaque types indistinguishable from empty object.
rounds.fix: Added CANON_TAG_WEAKMAP / WEAKSET / WEAKREF / ITERATOR sentinels. prepareForJson detects via instanceof + isIteratorLike(). Map/Set/Array/Date excluded from iterator branch to preserve their richer encoding.

rounds.result: VERIFIED no bug — this.x in constructor creates own enumerable, properly walked
VERIFIED no bug under current threat model — Object.keys returns own only by JS spec; prototype data not state-relevant. Acceptable constraint.
VERIFIED no bug — non-enumerable is by design out-of-band metadata, not state. Object.keys excludes.
VERIFIED no bug — enumerable: true still walked correctly
VERIFIED no bug — by-design exclusion of non-enumerable
VERIFIED no bug — Reflect.set sets prototype, Object.keys still ['x'] only

rounds.angle: Numeric string key vs integer key (JS coercion)
rounds.result: VERIFIED — both coerce to '5' string per JS spec; distinct only on value
VERIFIED — preserved as string key in canonical
VERIFIED — JS throws 'not extensible', frozen object stays frozen
VERIFIED — 1000 canonicalize calls of same input yield ONE unique hash; checksumFrame pure
